name: merge-requests
context: List, inspect, create, and merge GitLab merge requests with source and target branch aware API workflows
info: This skill covers merge request automation in GitLab: list merge requests in a project, inspect a specific MR by IID, create a new MR from a prepared source branch to a target branch, and merge an MR with SHA guards, squash, source branch cleanup, or auto-merge behavior. Agents use it after local Git work is pushed and before deployment or release automation proceeds. It is also the place to understand MR-specific pipeline behavior and merge-status checks.
triggers: list merge requests in a gitlab project
open an mr from a branch
body:
# GitLab Merge Requests

Merge requests are the review and merge unit for code changes in GitLab. Agents usually arrive here after using `git push` to publish a branch and after using [[gitlab/projects]] to resolve the project identifier.

```bash
python ./scripts/manage-merge-requests.py create group/project feature/refactor main 'Refactor deploy flow' \
  --description 'Splits deploy logic into reusable steps' \
  --remove-source-branch \
  --squash

python ./scripts/manage-merge-requests.py merge group/project 17 --sha 4f8a1b2c3d
```

Creation uses `POST /projects/:id/merge_requests` and requires `source_branch`, `target_branch`, and `title`. Merging uses `PUT /projects/:id/merge_requests/:merge_request_iid/merge`; use the optional `sha` guard to ensure only the reviewed commit set is merged. For merge-request-specific pipelines, load [[gitlab/pipelines]] and remember that they are separate from ordinary branch pipelines.

sections.title: List and inspect merge requests
sections.body: Project-scoped merge requests are listed with `GET /projects/:id/merge_requests`, commonly filtered by `state`, `source_branch`, `target_branch`, `reviewer_id`, or text search. The response includes `detailed_merge_status`, which is more useful than the legacy `merge_status` field for deciding whether an MR can actually merge.

sections.title: Create merge requests
sections.body: Creating an MR requires a source branch that already exists in the source project. Optional fields include assignees, reviewers, labels, `remove_source_branch`, `target_project_id`, and `squash`. If you need a draft MR, prefix the title with `Draft:` because draft state is encoded in the title on creation.

sections.title: Merge safely
sections.body: Merging uses `PUT /projects/:id/merge_requests/:merge_request_iid/merge` and supports `sha`, `squash`, `should_remove_source_branch`, and `auto_merge`. The `sha` parameter acts like optimistic locking: GitLab refuses the merge if the source branch head has changed since review. This is the safest default for autonomous agents that must avoid merging unreviewed commits.

sections.title: Merge request pipelines
sections.body: GitLab has a dedicated endpoint to create pipelines for a merge request, and those pipelines are distinct from ordinary branch pipelines. A MR pipeline often uses rules such as `$CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE == "merge_request_event"`. If an agent expects a branch pipeline but only MR rules exist, the pipeline will appear missing until the trigger source is corrected.

links.context: MR creation and merge require the correct token and project role
CI-driven merge automation may use job tokens instead of long-lived user tokens
Resolve the project path or ID before creating or merging an MR
Encoded project paths are a frequent source of MR API mistakes
Inspect branch and merge-request pipelines that gate MR merges
Failed MR pipelines often need a retry before merge can proceed
Branches must exist and be pushed before an MR can be opened
Rebasing is often required when the target branch moved

related.note: Merge actions need an authenticated token with merge rights on the target project
Source and target branches are the core inputs to merge request creation
Merge automation frequently waits on pipeline reruns or cancellation of obsolete runs
GitHub pull requests are the nearest equivalent on another platform

scripts.name: Manage GitLab Merge Requests
scripts.description: List, inspect, create, and merge GitLab merge requests

GITLAB_TOKEN.description: Preferred API token; use unless OAUTH_TOKEN or CI_JOB_TOKEN is provided instead

CI_JOB_TOKEN.description: Optional CI job token for in-pipeline automation

GITLAB_HTTP_DEBUG.description: Set to 1 to print request details to stderr

sha.description: Expected HEAD SHA used as a merge guard

depends.reason: Merge request operations require authenticated project access
MR operations are scoped to a project path or ID
